Feature_Description: This procedure is used for the case when the UE moves from one gNB-DU to another gNB-DU within the same gNB-CU during NR operation for LTM.
Feature_Objectives: To enable the gNB-CU to initiate LTM configuration for a UE moving between gNB-DUs.
To create a UE context and set up one or more data bearers in the candidate gNB-DU for LTM.
To allow the gNB-CU to provide LTM-specific configurations (e.g., CSI resource configuration, LTM configuration ID mapping list) to the candidate gNB-DU.
Feature_Implementation_Steps: 1. The UE sends a MeasurementReport message (L3 measurement result) to the source gNB-DU containing measurements of neighbouring cells. The source gNB-DU sends an UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER message conveying the received MeasurementReport message to the gNB-CU.
2. The gNB-CU determines to initiate LTM configuration.

3. The gNB-CU sends a UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message to the candidate gNB-DU(s) for each candidate cell, containing one candidate cell ID and the CSI resource configuration for subsequent LTM. The gNB-CU may provide the LTM configuration ID mapping list to the candidate gNB-DU(s). The gNB-CU may request PRACH resources from the candidate gNB-DU(s). The gNB-CU may request the candidate gNB-DU(s) to provide the lower layer configuration for the purpose of generating the reference configuration or provide the lower layer part of the reference configuration to the candidate gNB-DU(s).

4. If the candidate gNB-DU accepts the request of LTM configuration, it responds with a UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message including the generated lower layer RRC configurations for the accepted target candidate cell.
Procedure_Implementation_Steps: 1. The UE sends a MeasurementReport message (L3 measurement result) to the source gNB-DU, which forwards it to the gNB-CU via an UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER message.
2. The gNB-CU analyzes the MeasurementReport and determines to initiate LTM configuration for the UE.

3. The gNB-CU prepares and sends an F1AP UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message to the candidate gNB-DU(s) for each candidate cell. This message includes the candidate cell ID, CSI resource configuration for subsequent LTM, and may optionally include the LTM configuration ID mapping list, a request for PRACH resources, and/or the lower layer configuration (or part of it) for generating the reference configuration.

4. Upon receiving the UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST, the candidate gNB-DU processes the request. If it accepts the LTM configuration, it responds to the gNB-CU with an F1AP UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message. This response includes the generated lower layer RRC configurations for the accepted target candidate cell.

Code_Instructions.description: Data structure defining the format of the F1AP UE Context Setup Request message, used by the gNB-CU to initiate UE context setup at the gNB-DU for LTM handover.

Data structure defining the format of the F1AP UE Context Setup Response message, used by the gNB-DU to acknowledge and provide configuration details to the gNB-CU after receiving a UE Context Setup Request.
Helper function responsible for preparing and potentially encoding the F1AP UE Context Setup Request message from the RRC layer for transmission to the F1AP layer and subsequently to the gNB-DU.

Helper function within the gNB RRC layer responsible for generating the RRC-level information and parameters (e.g., CSI resource configuration, LTM configuration ID mapping, PRACH resources) required to populate the F1AP UE Context Setup Request message.

Helper function responsible for receiving and parsing the F1AP UE Context Setup Response message from the F1AP layer, extracting relevant information (e.g., lower layer RRC configurations) and forwarding it to the RRC layer for further processing.
